pubmed_links: [Reilly et al. (2024) Autism and attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder in children with Dravet syndrome: A population-based study](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38676322/); [Ricobaraza et al. (2019) Epilepsy and neuropsychiatric comorbidities in mice carrying a recurrent Dravet syndrome SCN1A missense mutation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31578435/); [Papp-Hertelendi et al. (2018) First report on the association of SCN1A mutation, childhood schizophrenia and autism spectrum disorder without epilepsy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30060894/)
summary: SCN1A encodes the Nav1.1 voltage-gated sodium channel alpha subunit; loss-of-function variants are the primary cause of Dravet syndrome, which carries high rates of autism spectrum disorder and intellectual disability, with rare SCN1A variants also identified in autism probands and childhood schizophrenia without epilepsy. (well-established)
